paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Cork - the thick bark of the cork oak tree (Quercus suber) - is a remarkable material. It is tough, elastic, buoyant, and fire-resistant, and suitable for a wide range of purposes. It has also been used for millennia: the ancient Egyptians sealed their sarcophagi (stone coffins) with cork, while the ancient Greeks and Romans used it for anything from beehives to sandals.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: And the cork oak itself is an extraordinary tree. Its bark grows up to 20 cm in thickness, insulating the tree like a coat wrapped around the trunk and branches and keeping the inside at a constant 20°C all year round. Developed most probably as a defence against forest fires, the bark of the cork oak has a particular cellular structure - with about 40 million cells per cubic centimetre - that technology has never succeeded in replicating. The cells are filled with air, which is why cork is so buoyant. It also has an elasticity that means you can squash it and watch it spring back to its original size and shape when you release the pressure.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: Cork oaks grow in a number of Mediterranean countries, including Portugal, Spain, Italy, Greece and Morocco. They flourish in warm, sunny climates where there is a minimum of 400 millimetres of rain per year, and not more than 800 millimetres. Like grape vines, the trees thrive in poor soil, putting down deep roots in search of moisture and nutrients. Southern Portugal's Alentejo region meets all of these requirements, which explains why, by the early 20th century, this region had become the world's largest producer of cork, and why today it accounts for roughly half of all cork production around the world.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Most cork forests are family-owned. Many of these family businesses, and indeed many of the trees themselves, are around 200 years old. Cork production is, above all, an exercise in patience. From the planting of a cork sapling to the first harvest takes 25 years, and a gap of approximately a decade must separate harvests from an individual tree. And for top-quality cork, it's necessary to wait a further 15 or 20 years. You even have to wait for the right kind of summer's day to harvest cork. If the bark is stripped on a day when it's too cold - or when the air is damp - the tree will be damaged.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: Cork harvesting is a very specialised profession. No mechanical means of stripping cork bark has been invented, so the job is done by teams of highly skilled workers. First, they make vertical cuts down the bark using small sharp axes, then lever it away in pieces as large as they can manage. The most skilful cork-strippers prise away a semi-circular husk that runs the length of the trunk from just above ground level to the first branches. It is then dried on the ground for about four months, before being taken to factories, where it is boiled to kill any insects that might remain in the cork. Over 60% of cork then goes on to be made into traditional bottle stoppers, with most of the remainder being used in the construction trade. Corkboard and cork tiles are ideal for thermal and acoustic insulation, while granules of cork are used in the manufacture of concrete.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: Recent years have seen the end of the virtual monopoly of cork as the material for bottle stoppers, due to concerns about the effect it may have on the contents of the bottle. This is caused by a chemical compound called 2,4,6-trichloroanisole (TCA), which forms through the interaction of plant phenols, chlorine and mould. The tiniest concentrations - as little as three or four parts to a trillion - can spoil the taste of the product contained in the bottle. The result has been a gradual yet steady move first towards plastic stoppers and, more recently, to aluminium screw caps. These substitutes are cheaper to manufacture and, in the case of screw caps, more convenient for the user.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: The classic cork stopper does have several advantages, however. Firstly, its traditional image is more in keeping with that of the type of high quality goods with which it has long been associated. Secondly - and very importantly - cork is a sustainable product that can be recycled without difficulty. Moreover, cork forests are a resource which support local biodiversity, and prevent desertification in the regions where they are planted. So, given the current concerns about environmental issues, the future of this ancient material once again looks promising.

groups.desc:
Questions 1-5
Do the following statements agree with the claims of the writer in Reading Passage 3?
Choose
TRUE if the statement agrees with the information given in the text,
FALSE if the statement contradicts the information,
NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.

questions.title: The cork oak has the thickest bark of any living tree.
questions.analysis: 题干中的线索词“thickest bark”和“any living tree”在原文中没有完全对应的同义替换词。虽然原文提到cork oak的树皮厚度为20厘米，但并没有提到这是所有树中最厚的。因此，题干中的信息在文章中找不到明确的对应，答案应为NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.sentence: Its bark grows up to 20 cm in thickness, insulating the tree like a coat wrapped around the trunk and branches and keeping the inside at a constant 20°C all year round.
central_sentences.translation: 它的树皮可以长到20厘米厚，像一件包裹在树干和树枝周围的外套一样隔热，全年保持内部温度恒定在20°C。

syn_ary.synonym: bark grows up to 20 cm in thickness

target_sen.target_sen: Its bark grows up to 20 cm in thickness, insulating the tree like a coat wrapped around the trunk and branches and keeping the inside at a constant 20°C all year round.

questions.title: Scientists have developed a synthetic cork with the same cellular structure as natural cork.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息是“科学家已经开发出具有相同细胞结构的合成软木”，而原文明确提到“技术从未成功复制软木的细胞结构”。这两者的信息是相反的，因此答案是FALSE。题干中的“developed”对应原文中的“succeeded in replicating”，“synthetic cork”对应“technology”，“same cellular structure”对应“particular cellular structure”。通过这些对应关系，可以确定题干信息与原文信息相矛盾。

central_sentences.sentence: the bark of the cork oak has a particular cellular structure - with about 40 million cells per cubic centimetre - that technology has never succeeded in replicating.
central_sentences.translation: 软木橡树的树皮有一种特殊的细胞结构——每立方厘米约有4000万个细胞——而技术从未成功复制这种结构。

target_sen.target_sen: Developed most probably as a defence against forest fires, the bark of the cork oak has a particular cellular structure - with about 40 million cells per cubic centimetre - that technology has never succeeded in replicating.

Developed most probably as a defence against forest fires, the bark of the cork oak has a particular cellular structure - with about 40 million cells per cubic centimetre - that technology has never succeeded in replicating.

Developed most probably as a defence against forest fires, the bark of the cork oak has a particular cellular structure - with about 40 million cells per cubic centimetre - that technology has never succeeded in replicating.

questions.title: Individual cork oak trees must be left for 25 years between the first and second harvest.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“Individual cork oak trees must be left for 25 years between the first and second harvest”意思是“第一次和第二次采收之间必须间隔25年”。而原文中明确提到“从种植软木树苗到第一次采收需要25年，而每次采收之间必须间隔大约十年”。这表明两次采收之间的间隔是十年，而不是25年。因此，题干信息与原文信息矛盾，正确答案是FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: From the planting of a cork sapling to the first harvest takes 25 years, and a gap of approximately a decade must separate harvests from an individual tree.
central_sentences.translation: 从种植软木树苗到第一次采收需要25年，而对于单棵树来说，每次采收之间必须间隔大约十年。

target_sen.target_sen: From the planting of a cork sapling to the first harvest takes 25 years, and a gap of approximately a decade must separate harvests from an individual tree.

questions.title: Cork bark should be stripped in dry atmospheric conditions.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "Cork bark should be stripped in dry atmospheric conditions" 意思是软木树皮应该在干燥的气候条件下被剥离。原文中提到 "If the bark is stripped on a day when it's too cold - or when the air is damp - the tree will be damaged"，这说明在潮湿的条件下剥离树皮会对树木造成损害，因此可以推断出干燥的条件是合适的。通过对比题干和原文，可以确认题干中的信息在文章中找到了对应的表达，因此答案是 TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: If the bark is stripped on a day when it's too cold - or when the air is damp - the tree will be damaged.
central_sentences.translation: 如果在天气太冷或空气潮湿的日子剥离树皮，树木会受到伤害。

target_sen.target_sen: If the bark is stripped on a day when it's too cold - or when the air is damp - the tree will be damaged.

central_sentences.sentence: Firstly, its traditional image is more in keeping with that of the type of high quality goods with which it has long been associated.

The classic cork stopper does have several advantages, however.
Secondly - and very importantly - cork is a sustainable product that can be recycled without difficulty.
Moreover, cork forests are a resource which support local biodiversity, and prevent desertification in the regions where they are planted.

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Collecting must be one of the most varied of human activities, and it's one that many of us psychologists find fascinating. Many forms of collecting have been dignified with a technical name: an arctophilist collects teddy bears, a philatelist collects postage stamps, and a deltiologist collects postcards. Amassing hundreds or even thousands of postcards, chocolate wrappers or whatever, takes time, energy and money that could surely be put to much more productive use. And yet there are millions of collectors around the world. Why do they do it?

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: There are the people who collect because they want to make money - this could be called an instrumental reason for collecting; that is, collecting as a means to an end. They'll look for, say, antiques that they can buy cheaply and expect to be able to sell at a profit. But there may well be a psychological element, too - buying cheap and selling dear can give the collector a sense of triumph. And as selling online is so easy, more and more people are joining in.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: Many collectors collect to develop their social life, attending meetings of a group of collectors and exchanging information on items. This is a variant on joining a bridge club or a gym, and similarly brings them into contact with like-minded people.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Another motive for collecting is the desire to find something special, or a particular example of the collected item, such as a rare early recording by a particular singer.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: Some may spend their whole lives in a hunt for this. Psychologically, this can give a purpose to a life that otherwise feels aimless. There is a danger, though, that if the individual is ever lucky enough to find what they're looking for, rather than celebrating their success, they may feel empty, now that the goal that drove them on has gone.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: If you think about collecting postage stamps, another potential reason for it - or, perhaps, a result of collecting - is its educational value. Stamp collecting opens a window to other countries, and to the plants, animals, or famous people shown on their stamps. Similarly, in the 19th century, many collectors amassed fossils, animals and plants from around the globe, and their collections provided a vast amount of information about the natural world. Without those collections, our understanding would be greatly inferior to what it is.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: In the past - and nowadays, too, though to a lesser extent - a popular form of collecting, particularly among boys and men, was trainspotting. This might involve trying to see every locomotive of a particular type, using published data that identifies each one, and ticking off each engine as it is seen. Train spotters exchange information, these days often by mobile phone, so they can work out where to go to, to see a particular engine. As a by-product, many practitioners of the hobby become very knowledgeable about railway operations, or the technical specifications of different engine types.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: Similarly, people who collect dolls may go beyond simply enlarging their collection, and develop an interest in the way that dolls are made, or the materials that are used. These have changed over the centuries from the wood that was standard in 16th century Europe, through the wax and porcelain of later centuries, to the plastics of today's dolls. Or collectors might be inspired to study how dolls reflect notions of what children like, or ought to like.

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text: Not all collectors are interested in learning from their hobby, though, so what we might call a psychological reason for collecting is the need for a sense of control, perhaps as a way of dealing with insecurity. Stamp collectors, for instance, arrange their stamps in albums, usually very neatly, organising their collection according to certain commonplace principles - perhaps by country in alphabetical order, or grouping stamps by what they depict - people, birds, maps, and so on.

paragraphs.label: J
paragraphs.text: One reason, conscious or not, for what someone chooses to collect is to show the collector's individualism. Someone who decides to collect something as unexpected as dog collars, for instance, may be conveying their belief that they must be interesting themselves. And believe it or not, there is at least one dog collar museum in existence, and it grew out of a personal collection.

paragraphs.label: K
paragraphs.text: Of course, all hobbies give pleasure, but the common factor in collecting is usually passion: pleasure is putting it far too mildly. More than most other hobbies, collecting can be totally engrossing, and can give a strong sense of personal fulfilment. To non-collectors it may appear an eccentric, if harmless, way of spending time, but potentially, collecting has a lot going for it.

central_sentences.translation: 有些人可能会花费一生的时间来寻找这个特殊物品。

central_sentences.sentence: Another motive for collecting is the desire to find something special, or a particular example of the collected item, such as a rare early recording by a particular singer.
central_sentences.translation: 收藏的另一个动机是渴望找到特殊的东西，或者收藏品中的某个特定样本，比如某位歌手的罕见早期录音。

target_sen.target_sen: Some may spend their whole lives in a hunt for this.
Another motive for collecting is the desire to find something special, or a particular example of the collected item, such as a rare early recording by a particular singer.

syn_ary.synonym: particular example of the collected item

target_sen.target_sen: Another motive for collecting is the desire to find something special, or a particular example of the collected item, such as a rare early recording by a particular singer.

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "Searching for something particular" 对应文章中的 "the desire to find something special, or a particular example of the collected item"。题干中的 "prevent people from feeling" 对应文章中的 "give a purpose to a life that otherwise feels"。题干中的 "life is completely" 对应文章中的 "life that otherwise feels"。通过这些同义替换词，我们可以定位到答案句 "Psychologically, this can give a purpose to a life that otherwise feels aimless."。因此，答案是 "aimless" 或 "empty"。这两个词都表示生活没有目标或意义，符合题干的要求。

central_sentences.sentence: Psychologically, this can give a purpose to a life that otherwise feels aimless.
central_sentences.translation: 从心理学角度来看，这可以给原本感觉毫无目标的生活赋予一个目的。

syn_ary.synonym: give a purpose to a life that otherwise feels

target_sen.target_sen: Psychologically, this can give a purpose to a life that otherwise feels aimless.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“Stamp collecting may be 【 】 because it provides facts about different countries”与原文中的“collecting postage stamps”和“opens a window to other countries”是同义表达。通过识别这些同义表达，可以定位到正确的答案是“educational”。“educational value”指的是教育价值，意味着通过集邮可以学习到关于不同国家的信息。因此，答案是“educational”。

central_sentences.sentence: If you think about collecting postage stamps, another potential reason for it - or, perhaps, a result of collecting - is its educational value. Stamp collecting opens a window to other countries, and to the plants, animals, or famous people shown on their stamps.
central_sentences.translation: 如果你考虑集邮，它的另一个潜在原因——或者说，收集的结果——是它的教育价值。集邮为我们打开了解其他国家的窗口，让我们了解邮票上展示的植物、动物或名人。

target_sen.target_sen: If you think about collecting postage stamps, another potential reason for it - or, perhaps, a result of collecting - is its educational value.

syn_ary.synonym: opens a window to other countries

target_sen.target_sen: Stamp collecting opens a window to other countries, and to the plants, animals, or famous people shown on their stamps.

questions.analysis: 答案是 "Trainspotting"。题干中的 "male hobby" 对应原文中的 "boys and men"，而 "hobby" 对应 "form of collecting"。原文明确指出火车观察（trainspotting）特别受男孩和男人欢迎，因此答案是 "Trainspotting"。

central_sentences.sentence: In the past - and nowadays, too, though to a lesser extent - a popular form of collecting, particularly among boys and men, was trainspotting.
central_sentences.translation: 在过去，以及现在（尽管程度较小），一种流行的收藏形式，特别是在男孩和男人中间的，是火车观察。

target_sen.target_sen: In the past - and nowadays, too, though to a lesser extent - a popular form of collecting, particularly among boys and men, was trainspotting.

groups.desc:
Questions 22-26

Do the following statements agree with the claims of the writer in Reading Passage 2?
Choose
TRUE if the statement agrees with the information given in the text,
FALSE if the statement contradicts the information,
NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.

questions.title: The number of people buying dolls has grown over the centuries.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息是“购买娃娃的人数随着时间的推移而增加”，而原文中提到的是娃娃制作材料的变化和收藏者的兴趣变化，并没有提到购买娃娃的人数是否增加。因此，答案是 NOT GIVEN，因为文章中没有提供关于购买人数变化的信息。

central_sentences.sentence: Similarly, people who collect dolls may go beyond simply enlarging their collection, and develop an interest in the way that dolls are made, or the materials that are used. These have changed over the centuries from the wood that was standard in 16th century Europe, through the wax and porcelain of later centuries, to the plastics of today's dolls.
central_sentences.translation: 同样，收集娃娃的人可能不仅仅是扩大他们的收藏，还可能会对娃娃的制作方式或使用的材料产生兴趣。这些材料随着世纪的变迁而改变，从16世纪欧洲标准的木材，到后来几个世纪的蜡和瓷器，再到今天的塑料娃娃。

target_sen.target_sen: Similarly, people who collect dolls may go beyond simply enlarging their collection, and develop an interest in the way that dolls are made, or the materials that are used.
These have changed over the centuries from the wood that was standard in 16th century Europe, through the wax and porcelain of later centuries, to the plastics of today's dolls.

questions.title: Sixteenth century European dolls were normally made of wax and porcelain.
questions.analysis: 题干说16世纪的欧洲玩偶通常是用蜡和瓷做的，而文章明确提到16世纪欧洲的玩偶主要是用木头做的，蜡和瓷是后来的材料。因此，题干信息与文章内容相反，正确答案是FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: These have changed over the centuries from the wood that was standard in 16th century Europe, through the wax and porcelain of later centuries, to the plastics of today's dolls.
central_sentences.translation: 这些（材料）随着时间的推移而发生了变化，从16世纪欧洲标准的木材，到后来几个世纪的蜡和瓷，再到今天的塑料玩偶。

target_sen.target_sen: These have changed over the centuries from the wood that was standard in 16th century Europe, through the wax and porcelain of later centuries, to the plastics of today's dolls.
Similarly, people who collect dolls may go beyond simply enlarging their collection, and develop an interest in the way that dolls are made, or the materials that are used.
These have changed over the centuries from the wood that was standard in 16th century Europe, through the wax and porcelain of later centuries, to the plastics of today's dolls.
Or collectors might be inspired to study how dolls reflect notions of what children like, or ought to like.
These have changed over the centuries from the wood that was standard in 16th century Europe, through the wax and porcelain of later centuries, to the plastics of today's dolls.

questions.title: Arranging a stamp collection by the size of the stamps is less common than other methods.
questions.analysis: 题干中的线索词“arranging”、“stamp collection”、“size”、“less common”和“methods”在原文中都有对应的同义替换词。然而，原文中提到的常见整理方法包括按国家的字母顺序排列和按邮票上描绘的内容分组，但并没有提到按邮票大小排列。因此，无法判断按大小排列是否比其他方法更不常见，答案为NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.sentence: Stamp collectors, for instance, arrange their stamps in albums, usually very neatly, organising their collection according to certain commonplace principles - perhaps by country in alphabetical order, or grouping stamps by what they depict - people, birds, maps, and so on.
central_sentences.translation: 例如，邮票收藏者通常会将他们的邮票整齐地排列在集邮册中，根据一些常见的原则来组织他们的收藏——可能按国家的字母顺序排列，或者按邮票上描绘的内容（如人物、鸟类、地图等）进行分组。

target_sen.target_sen: Stamp collectors, for instance, arrange their stamps in albums, usually very neatly, organising their collection according to certain commonplace principles - perhaps by country in alphabetical order, or grouping stamps by what they depict - people, birds, maps, and so on.

Stamp collectors, for instance, arrange their stamps in albums, usually very neatly, organising their collection according to certain commonplace principles - perhaps by country in alphabetical order, or grouping stamps by what they depict - people, birds, maps, and so on.

questions.title: Someone who collects unusual objects may want others to think he or she is also unusual.
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是 TRUE，因为题干中的信息在原文中得到了明确的对应。题干中的 "collects unusual objects" 对应原文中的 "collect something as unexpected as dog collars"，而 "want others to think he or she is also unusual" 对应原文中的 "conveying their belief that they must be interesting themselves"。这表明收集不寻常物品的人可能希望别人认为他们也很不寻常。因此，题干的陈述与原文内容一致。

central_sentences.sentence: Someone who decides to collect something as unexpected as dog collars, for instance, may be conveying their belief that they must be interesting themselves.
central_sentences.translation: 例如，有人决定收集像狗项圈这样意想不到的物品，可能是在传达他们认为自己一定很有趣的信念。

syn_ary.synonym: something as unexpected as dog collars

target_sen.target_sen: Someone who decides to collect something as unexpected as dog collars, for instance, may be conveying their belief that they must be interesting themselves.

syn_ary.synonym: conveying their belief that they must be interesting themselves

target_sen.target_sen: Someone who decides to collect something as unexpected as dog collars, for instance, may be conveying their belief that they must be interesting themselves.

locating_words.locating_word: want others to think he or she is also unusual

questions.title: Collecting gives a feeling that other hobbies are unlikely to inspire.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息在文章中可以找到对应的表达。题干说“收集带来的感觉是其他爱好不太可能激发的”，而文章中提到“比大多数其他爱好更能让人全身心投入，并能带来强烈的个人成就感”，这说明收集带来的感觉确实是其他爱好不太可能激发的。因此，正确答案是 TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: Of course, all hobbies give pleasure, but the common factor in collecting is usually passion: pleasure is putting it far too mildly. More than most other hobbies, collecting can be totally engrossing, and can give a strong sense of personal fulfilment.
central_sentences.translation: 当然，所有的爱好都能带来快乐，但收集的共同特点通常是激情：说快乐太轻描淡写了。相比大多数其他爱好，收集可以让人更加全身心投入，并能带来强烈的个人成就感。

target_sen.target_sen: Of course, all hobbies give pleasure, but the common factor in collecting is usually passion: pleasure is putting it far too mildly.
More than most other hobbies, collecting can be totally engrossing, and can give a strong sense of personal fulfilment.
To non-collectors it may appear an eccentric, if harmless, way of spending time, but potentially, collecting has a lot going for it.
Of course, all hobbies give pleasure, but the common factor in collecting is usually passion: pleasure is putting it far too mildly.
More than most other hobbies, collecting can be totally engrossing, and can give a strong sense of personal fulfilment.
More than most other hobbies, collecting can be totally engrossing, and can give a strong sense of personal fulfilment.
Of course, all hobbies give pleasure, but the common factor in collecting is usually passion: pleasure is putting it far too mildly.
More than most other hobbies, collecting can be totally engrossing, and can give a strong sense of personal fulfilment.

content.title: What's the purpose of gaining knowledge?
content.summary: {'A': '康奈尔大学的创始人的座右铭是“我将创办一个任何人都可以在任何学科中找到教育的机构”，这也适用于我目前所在的这所美国大学，学生可以准备从事度假村管理、工程、室内设计、会计、音乐、执法等各种职业，但是这两所学校的创始人会对一个名为“以纵火牟利”的课程有何看法呢？我没有开玩笑：我们的课程中确实有这门课程，只要本科生符合学术要求，就可以报名参加我们的“火灾科学”项目。', 'B': '这段话主要讲述了火灾调查员课程的内容，以及对于潜在纵火犯来说，这也是一个完美的课程选择。作者并不是批评火灾科学学术项目，而是指出不诚实和非法行为如何通过高等教育渗透到公共和商业生活的方方面面。\n\n简短总结：火灾调查员课程既适合潜在调查员学习火灾调查技巧，也可能吸引潜在纵火犯，同时也提醒人们高等教育如何影响公共和商业生活中的不诚实和非法行为。', 'C': '这段话主要讲述了作者在一堂市场营销课上提出了一个问题：“市场营销是否有原则？”并且许多学生认为市场营销不需要遵循道德原则。', 'D': '这段话主要讲述了作者提出了一个可能听起来完全疯狂的建议，即市场营销本质上是有原则的，并以哲学家康德为例来支持这个观点。', 'E': '这段话主要讲述了关于市场营销的目的和意义的讨论，提出了两种不同的态度，一种认为市场营销的目的是明显的，即销售产品和赚钱；另一种态度认为市场营销的目的是无关紧要的，每个人参加课程都有自己的计划，不一定需要关注市场营销技能的获取。作者提出了自己的观点，即市场营销的目的和手段都应该受到重视，学生需要学习如何实现目标X，同时也需要了解目标X是什么。', 'F': '这段话主要讲述了火灾科学学生在学习如何检测和起诉犯罪活动的过程中，他们需要明确他们所学领域的目标和目的，以确保其伦理性，同时强调了使用相同的知识和技能来实现不同的目标会有不同的结果和评价。简单总结为：火灾科学学生需要明确自己的目标和目的，以确保其伦理性，同时强调了使用相同的知识和技能来实现不同的目标会有不同的结果和评价。'}

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: 'I would found an institution where any person can find instruction in any subject.' That was the founder's motto for Cornell University, and it seems an apt characterization of the different university, also in the USA, where I currently teach philosophy. A student can prepare for a career in resort management, engineering, interior design, accounting, music, law enforcement, you name it. But what would the founders of these two institutions have thought of a course called 'Arson for Profit'? I kid you not: we have it on the books. Any undergraduates who have met the academic requirements can sign up for the course in our program in 'fire science'.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: Naturally, the course is intended for prospective arson investigators, who can learn all the tricks of the trade for detecting whether a fire was deliberately set, discovering who did it, and establishing a chain of evidence for effective prosecution in a court of law. But wouldn't this also be the perfect course for prospective arsonists to sign up for? My point is not to criticize academic programs in fire science: they are highly welcome as part of the increasing professionalization of this and many other occupations. However, it's not unknown for a firefighter to torch a building. This example suggests how dishonest and illegal behavior, with the help of higher education, can creep into every aspect of public and business life.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: I realized this anew when I was invited to speak before a class in marketing, which is another of our degree programs. The regular instructor is a colleague who appreciates the kind of ethical perspective I can bring as a philosopher. There are endless ways I could have approached this assignment, but I took my cue from the title of the course: 'Principles of Marketing'. It made me think to ask the students, 'Is marketing principled?' After all, a subject matter can have principles in the sense of being codified, having rules, as with football or chess, without being principled in the sense of being ethical. Many of the students immediately assumed that the answer to my question about marketing principles was obvious: no. Just look at the ways in which everything under the sun has been marketed; obviously it need not be done in a principled (=ethical) fashion.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Is that obvious? I made the suggestion, which may sound downright crazy in light of the evidence, that perhaps marketing is by definition principled. My inspiration for this judgement is the philosopher Immanuel Kant, who argued that any body of knowledge consists of an end (or purpose) and a means.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: Let us apply both the terms 'means' and 'end' to marketing. The students have signed up for a course in order to learn how to market effectively. But to what end? There seem to be two main attitudes toward that question. One is that the answer is obvious: the purpose of marketing is to sell things and to make money. The other attitude is that the purpose of marketing is irrelevant: Each person comes to the program and course with his or her own plans, and these need not even concern the acquisition of marketing expertise as such. My proposal, which I believe would also be Kant's, is that neither of these attitudes captures the significance of the end to the means for marketing. A field of knowledge or a professional endeavor is defined by both the means and the end; hence both deserve scrutiny. Students need to study both how to achieve X, and also what X is.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: It is at this point that 'Arson for Profit' becomes supremely relevant. That course is presumably all about means: how to detect and prosecute criminal activity. It is therefore assumed that the end is good in an ethical sense. When I ask fire science students to articulate the end, or purpose, of their field, they eventually generalize to something like, 'The safety and welfare of society,' which seems right. As we have seen, someone could use the very same knowledge of means to achieve a much less noble end, such as personal profit via destructive, dangerous, reckless activity. But we would not call that firefighting. We have a separate word for it: arson. Similarly, if you employed the 'principles of marketing' in an unprincipled way, you would not be doing marketing. We have another term for it: fraud. Kant gives the example of a doctor and a poisoner, who use the identical knowledge to achieve their divergent ends. We would say that one is practicing medicine, the other, murder.

groups.title: List of Headings
groups.desc:
Questions 27-32
The text has 6 sections.
Choose the correct heading for each section and move it into the gap.

list.text: Courses that require a high level of commitment
A course title with two meanings
The equal importance of two key issues
Applying a theory in an unexpected context
The possibility of attracting the wrong kind of student

questions.analysis: Section A的主要内容是介绍一门名为“Arson for Profit”的课程，这个课程标题令人惊讶。文章中明确提到“a course called 'Arson for Profit'”和“kid you not”，这两个短语直接对应了小标题vi中的“a surprising course title”。因此，Section A的最佳标题是vi。

central_sentences.sentence: But what would the founders of these two institutions have thought of a course called 'Arson for Profit'？I kid you not: we have it on the books. Any
central_sentences.translation: 但是这两所大学的创始人会怎么看待一门名为"纵火牟利"的课程呢？我可不是在开玩笑：我们的课程表上确实有这门课。

target_sen.target_sen: But what would the founders of these two institutions have thought of a course called 'Arson for Profit'？I kid you not: we have it on the books.
But what would the founders of these two institutions have thought of a course called 'Arson for Profit'？I kid you not: we have it on the books.

questions.analysis: Section B的主要内容是讨论“Arson for Profit”这门课程可能会吸引潜在的纵火犯，这与小标题viii: The possibility of attracting the wrong kind of student完全对应。通过识别文章中的关键词和理解文章的逻辑，学生可以正确选择这个小标题。

central_sentences.sentence: But wouldn't this also be the perfect course for prospective arsonists to sign up for?
central_sentences.translation: 但这难道不也是潜在纵火犯报名的理想课程吗？

target_sen.target_sen: But wouldn't this also be the perfect course for prospective arsonists to sign up for?

questions.analysis: C 段前三句都是客观事实铺垫，不具备段落主旨句的特征，因此不用从这三句里找解题依据。段落第四句抛出核心设问：is marketing principled？（市场营销是否有原则？）按照常规行文逻辑，后文会围绕这个问题展开解释说明。后文提到：一门学科（对应课程名称 a course title），可以在规则编纂层面有原则，就像足球、象棋有固定规则一样；但在道德伦理层面，却未必恪守原则。句中 in the sense of being codified 和 in the sense of being ethical，刚好对应两种不同含义；a subject matter 也同义替换了 a course title。综上，正确答案为 B：A course title with two meanings。

central_sentences.sentence: Naturally, the course is intended for prospective arson investigators, who can learn all the tricks of the trade for detecting whether a fire was deliberately set, discovering who did it, and establishing a chain of evidence for effective prosecution in a court of law. But wouldn't this also be the perfect course for prospective arsonists to sign up for?
central_sentences.translation: 当然，这门课程是为未来的纵火调查员设计的，他们可以学习所有的专业技巧，用于检测火灾是否是蓄意纵火，发现纵火者，并建立有效的证据链以便在法庭上进行起诉。但是，这不也是未来纵火犯报名的理想课程吗？

target_sen.target_sen: Naturally, the course is intended for prospective arson investigators, who can learn all the tricks of the trade for detecting whether a fire was deliberately set, discovering who did it, and establishing a chain of evidence for effective prosecution in a court of law.
But wouldn't this also be the perfect course for prospective arsonists to sign up for?

questions.analysis: Section D的主要内容是作者将康德的理论应用到市场营销中，探讨市场营销是否有原则（ethical principles）。作者提出了一个看似疯狂的观点，即市场营销本质上是有原则的，并通过康德的理论进行解释。这正是将一个理论应用到一个意想不到的情境中的典型例子。因此，iv: Applying a theory in an unexpected context是最合适的小标题。

central_sentences.sentence: My inspiration for this judgement is the philosopher Immanuel Kant, who argued that any body of knowledge consists of an end (or purpose) and a means.
central_sentences.translation: 我对这个判断的灵感来自哲学家伊曼纽尔·康德，他认为任何知识体系都包含一个目的（或目标）和一种手段。

central_sentences.sentence: Let us apply both the terms 'means' and 'end' to marketing.
central_sentences.translation: 让我们将"手段"和"目的"这两个术语应用到市场营销中。

target_sen.target_sen: My inspiration for this judgement is the philosopher Immanuel Kant, who argued that any body of knowledge consists of an end (or purpose) and a means.
My proposal, which I believe would also be Kants, is that neither of these attitudes captures the significance of the end to the means for marketing.
I made the suggestion, which may sound downright crazy in light of the evidence, that perhaps marketing is by definition principled.
Let us apply both the terms 'means' and 'end' to marketing.
One is that the answer is obvious: the purpose of marketing is to sell things and to make money.

The other attitude is that the purpose of marketing is irrelevant: Each person comes to the program and course with his or her own plans, and these need not even concern the acquisition of marketing expertise as such.
My proposal, which I believe would also be Kants, is that neither of these attitudes captures the significance of the end to the means for marketing.
I made the suggestion, which may sound downright crazy in light of the evidence, that perhaps marketing is by definition principled.
Let us apply both the terms 'means' and 'end' to marketing.
One is that the answer is obvious: the purpose of marketing is to sell things and to make money.

The other attitude is that the purpose of marketing is irrelevant: Each person comes to the program and course with his or her own plans, and these need not even concern the acquisition of marketing expertise as such.
My proposal, which I believe would also be Kants, is that neither of these attitudes captures the significance of the end to the means for marketing.

questions.analysis: Section E的主要讨论内容是“means”和“end”这两个关键问题的同等重要性。文章中明确提到“a field of knowledge or a professional endeavor is defined by both the means and the end; hence both deserve scrutiny”，这与标题iii中的“equal importance of two key issues”直接对应。因此，Section E的最佳标题是iii。

central_sentences.sentence: My proposal, which I believe would also be Kants, is that neither of these attitudes captures the significance of the end to the means for marketing. A field of knowledge or a professional endeavor is defined by both the means and the end; hence both deserve scrutiny.
central_sentences.translation: 我的建议，我相信这也是康德的观点，是这两种态度都没有抓住营销中目的对手段的重要性。一个知识领域或专业实践是由目的和手段共同定义的；因此两者都值得仔细审查。

questions.analysis: 这段文字强调了不同的结果（outcomes）有不同的名称（names），这与小标题 vii: Different names for different outcomes 完全一致。文章通过举例说明了相同的知识可以用于不同的目的，并且这些不同的目的有不同的名称，如“纵火”和“欺诈”。

central_sentences.sentence: We have a separate word for it: arson. Similarly, if you employed the 'principles of marketing' in an unprincipled way, you would not be doing marketing. We have another term for it: fraud.
central_sentences.translation: 我们对此有一个专门的词：纵火。同样，如果你以不道德的方式运用"营销原则"，那就不是在做营销。我们有另一个词来形容它：欺诈。

target_sen.target_sen: We have a separate word for it: arson.
We have another term for it: fraud.
Kant gives the example of a doctor and a poisoner, who use the identical knowledge to achieve their divergent ends.

groups.title: The 'Arson for Profit' course
groups.desc:
Questions 33-36
Complete the summary below.
Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS in each gap.
groups.collect: This is a university course intended for students who are undergraduates and who are studying $987$. The expectation is that they will become $988$specialising in arson. The course will help them to detect cases of arson and find $989$of criminal intent, leading to successful $990$in the courts.

questions.analysis: 题干中的关键词是“undergraduates”和“studying”，在原文中对应的表达是“undergraduates”和“program in 'fire science'”。这两个表达直接对应，说明这个课程是为本科生开设的，且他们学习的专业是“fire science”。因此，正确答案是“fire science”。

central_sentences.sentence: Any undergraduates who have met the academic requirements can sign up for the course in our program in 'fire science'.
central_sentences.translation: 任何满足学术要求的本科生都可以报名参加我们"消防科学"专业的课程。

target_sen.target_sen: Any undergraduates who have met the academic requirements can sign up for the course in our program in 'fire science'.
Any undergraduates who have met the academic requirements can sign up for the course in our program in 'fire science'.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“specialising in arson”对应原文中的“arson investigators”。原文明确提到课程是为“prospective arson investigators”准备的，这些调查员将专门从事纵火调查工作。因此，答案是“investigators”。

central_sentences.sentence: Naturally, the course is intended for prospective arson investigators, who can learn all the tricks of the trade for detecting whether a fire was deliberately set, discovering who did it, and establishing a chain of evidence for effective prosecution in a court of law.
central_sentences.translation: 自然而然，这门课程是为未来的纵火调查员设计的，他们可以学习各种技巧，用于检测火灾是否是蓄意纵火，发现纵火者，并建立有效的证据链以便在法庭上进行起诉。

target_sen.target_sen: Naturally, the course is intended for prospective arson investigators, who can learn all the tricks of the trade for detecting whether a fire was deliberately set, discovering who did it, and establishing a chain of evidence for effective prosecution in a court of law.

questions.analysis: 题干中的"detect cases of arson"对应原文中的"detecting whether a fire was deliberately set"，"find ... of criminal intent"对应"discovering who did it"和"establishing a chain of evidence"，"leading to successful prosecution in the courts"对应"for effective prosecution in a court of law"。通过这些对应关系，我们可以确定答案是"evidence"，因为它是原文中明确提到的用于起诉的关键要素。

central_sentences.sentence: Naturally, the course is intended for prospective arson investigators, who can learn all the tricks of the trade for detecting whether a fire was deliberately set, discovering who did it, and establishing a chain of evidence for effective prosecution in a court of law.
central_sentences.translation: 当然，这门课程是为未来的纵火案调查员设计的，他们可以学习所有的专业技巧，用于检测火灾是否是蓄意纵火，发现纵火者，并建立证据链以在法庭上进行有效起诉。

syn_ary.synonym: detecting whether a fire was deliberately set

target_sen.target_sen: Naturally, the course is intended for prospective arson investigators, who can learn all the tricks of the trade for detecting whether a fire was deliberately set, discovering who did it, and establishing a chain of evidence for effective prosecution in a court of law.

questions.analysis: 答案 "prosecution" 是正确的，因为题干中的 "leading to successful 【 】in the courts" 对应原文中的 "effective prosecution in a court of law"。通过对比题干和原文，可以发现 "prosecution" 是最合适的填空词。题干中的 "detect cases of arson" 和 "find of criminal intent" 分别对应原文中的 "detecting whether a fire was deliberately set" 和 "discovering who did it"，这些线索词帮助我们定位到答案句。

central_sentences.sentence: Naturally, the course is intended for prospective arson investigators, who can learn all the tricks of the trade for detecting whether a fire was deliberately set, discovering who did it, and establishing a chain of evidence for effective prosecution in a court of law.
central_sentences.translation: 当然，这门课程是为未来的纵火案调查员设计的。他们可以学习所有的专业技巧，包括如何检测火灾是否是蓄意纵火，发现纵火者是谁，以及建立有效的证据链，以便在法庭上进行有效的起诉。

syn_ary.synonym: detecting whether a fire was deliberately set

target_sen.target_sen: Naturally, the course is intended for prospective arson investigators, who can learn all the tricks of the trade for detecting whether a fire was deliberately set, discovering who did it, and establishing a chain of evidence for effective prosecution in a court of law.

Naturally, the course is intended for prospective arson investigators, who can learn all the tricks of the trade for detecting whether a fire was deliberately set, discovering who did it, and establishing a chain of evidence for effective prosecution in a court of law.

groups.desc:
Questions 37-40
Do the following statements agree with the claims of the writer in Reading Passage 3?
Choose
YES if the statement agrees with the claims of the writer,
NO if the statement contradicts the claims of the writer,
NOT GIVEN if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this.

questions.title: It is difficult to attract students onto courses that do not focus on a career.
questions.analysis: 题干中的线索词“difficult”、“attract students”和“courses that do not focus on a career”在原文中没有找到对应的同义替换词或相关信息。原文主要讨论了职业课程和特定课程（如“Arson for Profit”），但没有提到关于“难以吸引学生”这方面的信息。因此，正确答案是NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.reason: 文中未讨论课程是否以职业为导向或招生难度。
central_sentences.analysis: 原文未提非职业导向课程是否难吸引学生。

questions.title: The 'Arson for Profit' course would be useful for people intending to set fire to buildings.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息在文章中可以找到对应的表达。题干中的“Arson for Profit”在原文中直接提到；“useful”在原文中对应“perfect course”；“people intending to set fire to buildings”在原文中对应“prospective arsonists”。因此，题干陈述的信息在文章中得到了明确的支持，正确答案是 YES。

central_sentences.sentence: But wouldn't this also be the perfect course for prospective arsonists to sign up for?
central_sentences.translation: 但是这不也是一个很适合未来纵火犯报名的完美课程吗？

target_sen.target_sen: But wouldn't this also be the perfect course for prospective arsonists to sign up for?
But wouldn't this also be the perfect course for prospective arsonists to sign up for?

locating_words.locating_word: people intending to set fire to buildings

questions.title: Fire science courses are too academic to help people to be good at the job of firefighting.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“too academic”在原文中对应的同义替换词是“highly welcome”，而“help people to be good at the job of firefighting”在原文中对应的同义替换词是“intended for prospective arson investigators”和“learn all the tricks of the trade for detecting whether a fire was deliberately set”。原文明确表示火科学课程是为了帮助未来的纵火调查员学习相关技能，并且作者认为这些课程是职业化的一部分，因此题干中的说法与文章内容相反，正确答案是NO。

central_sentences.sentence: My point is not to criticize academic programs in fire science: they are highly welcome as part of the increasing professionalization of this and many other occupations.
central_sentences.translation: 我的观点并非批评火科学的学术课程：它们作为这个以及许多其他职业日益专业化的一部分是非常受欢迎的。

target_sen.target_sen: My point is not to criticize academic programs in fire science: they are highly welcome as part of the increasing professionalization of this and many other occupations.
My point is not to criticize academic programs in fire science: they are highly welcome as part of the increasing professionalization of this and many other occupations.

syn_ary.synonym: learn all the tricks of the trade

target_sen.target_sen: Naturally, the course is intended for prospective arson investigators, who can learn all the tricks of the trade for detecting whether a fire was deliberately set, discovering who did it, and establishing a chain of evidence for effective prosecution in a court of law.

questions.title: The writer's fire science students provided a detailed definition of the purpose of their studies.
questions.analysis: 题干中提到“detailed definition”，意思是详细的定义。而原文中提到学生们“eventually generalize to something like”，意思是最终概括为某种类似的东西。这里的“generalize”与“detailed”是相反的，表明学生们提供的是一个概括性的定义，而不是详细的定义。因此，题干中的信息与原文中的信息是相反的，所以正确答案是“NO”。

central_sentences.sentence: When I ask fire science students to articulate the end, or purpose, of their field, they eventually generalize to something like, 'The safety and welfare of society,' which seems right.
central_sentences.translation: 当我要求消防科学的学生阐述他们领域的目标或目的时，他们最终概括为类似"社会的安全和福利"这样的说法，这看起来是正确的。

target_sen.target_sen: When I ask fire science students to articulate the end, or purpose, of their field, they eventually generalize to something like, 'The safety and welfare of society,' which seems right.

syn_ary.synonym: end, or purpose, of their field

target_sen.target_sen: When I ask fire science students to articulate the end, or purpose, of their field, they eventually generalize to something like, 'The safety and welfare of society,' which seems right.
